analysis.btc_price_note: BTC ~$78,210 on May 3 (up ~1.89% in 24h from May 2 close of $78,324). 31% rally off yearly lows, breaking multi-month descending channel. Key resistance: $80K psychological level not yet broken. Market cap ~$1.57T. Bitcoin dominance ~58.2%.
sentiment_key_signals: BTC +1.89% in 24h (~$78,210-78,324) — multi-month descending channel breakout confirmed
April spot BTC ETF monthly inflows $2.44B — strongest month since October 2025
May 1 daily ETF net inflows $629.8M: BlackRock IBIT $284.4M, Fidelity FBTC $213.4M
Ethereum ETFs returned to inflows May 1: $101.2M net
US-Iran standoff: US maintaining naval blockade; Iran demands blockade lifted before reopening Strait of Hormuz — geopolitical risk unresolved
Goldman Sachs filed SEC registration for Bitcoin Premium Income ETF — institutional expansion
SEC approved generic ETF listing standards (faster approval — up to 75 days vs 240 days)
SEC reviewing NYSE Arca proposal requiring 85% NAV in pre-approved assets for crypto ETPs
FOMC held 3.5-3.75% at April 29 meeting; next FOMC June 16-17
Powell Fed chairmanship ends May 15; Kevin Warsh nominated as next Fed Chair — transition risk
Ark Invest projects BTC market cap $16T by 2030; Standard Chartered targets $150K for 2026
Consensus 2026 conference Miami May 5-7 — upcoming sentiment catalyst
Market is selective: broad altcoin breakout NOT confirmed; large-caps in narrow range
analysis.macro_backdrop: Weekend May 3 — no scheduled macro data releases. FOMC April 29 held at 3.5-3.75%; Fed projects median one cut in 2026 but 7 members forecast zero cuts. CPI at 3.3% YoY (above 2% target). Next key data: ISM Services May 5 (Monday), NFP April data May 8, CPI/PPI ~May 12-13. Powell exit May 15 adds leadership uncertainty. Iran-US Strait of Hormuz standoff is key geopolitical risk — any escalation = sharp BTC selloff possible.
analysis.regime_rationale: Bullish (0.60): BTC maintaining breakout above $77.5K former resistance-turned-support after 31% rally off lows. ETF institutional demand strong ($2.44B April). However: (1) $80K resistance not broken, (2) US-Iran standoff unresolved — prior 'peace proposal' headline walked back, blockade ongoing, (3) weekend low liquidity = gap risk, (4) market rally is selective not broad-based. Confidence 0.60 — macro filter ACTIVE at this level.
